headline: Week three: Finland race week, vert falls well short
narrative: Race week in Finland reshaped this one entirely. The plan asked for the longest hike yet plus downhill volume; what actually happened was travel to Lahti, a parkrun shakeout, and Ultra X Finland — the 50K that wasn't to be, run instead at a shorter distance, though still comfortably the longest and hardest outing of the block. The vert came in well short of target, the first miss after two weeks of surpluses, with on-foot work essentially two sessions. A couple of rows and a bike explore of Lahti kept the engine ticking over.

The real headline came out of the race itself: the calf held up through the biggest effort yet — three weeks now with nothing worse than that opening twinge. The distance jump clearly bit, by your own account, which makes next week's shape convenient. Week four is a deload, and the ask is to absorb the work, not claw back the missed vert — on a recovery week, overshooting the target is the failure. Ease right off, let everything below the waist settle, and arrive at week five's steeper vert fresh.
